vibe: Minimal monochrome with one bold accent color. Premium, encouraging, and highly intentional. It should feel like a high-end editorial assessment rather than a generic template quiz.

typography.h1: text-5xl md:text-6xl font-black tracking-tighter text-text_primary leading-tight
typography.body_large: text-lg md:text-xl font-medium font-secondary text-text_secondary leading-relaxed

spacing_and_layout.surface_style: Flat with sharp 1px borders (border-border_subtle). No soft dropshadows. High contrast 'neobrutalism-lite' approach.
spacing_and_layout.alignment: Left-aligned for all reading content and forms. Center alignment ONLY for the final success message or empty states.

motion.page_transitions: Smooth fade in and slight slide up (Framer Motion).
motion.hover_states: Interactive cards should translate-y by -4px, borders change to accent_primary, and apply a hard, unblurred drop-shadow (e.g., shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#0038FF]).
motion.progress_bar: Animated width expansion with a sleek spring transition.

iconography.style: Duotone or Regular weight. Sharp, legible.

components_strategy.buttons: Shadcn based. Primary buttons must be solid accent_primary with white text. Generous padding (h-14, px-8).
components_strategy.cards: Custom built using HTML/Tailwind for max flexibility. Use 1px borders. Add internal padding p-6 or p-8.
components_strategy.progress_tracker: A fixed top or floating pill progress bar showing 'Question X of Y' and a visual percentage fill in accent_primary.
components_strategy.star_rating: Use Phosphor Icons (Star). Inactive: text-border_subtle. Active: text-accent_primary (Cobalt Blue). Add scale animation on click.

landing_page.layout: Asymmetrical split. Left side: Massive H1, minimal subheading, large primary CTA. Right side: Hero imagery overlapping a geometric shape.
landing_page.visuals: Apply a subtle grayscale to photos and use the accent_primary as a duotone or geometric overlay for a highly branded look.

assessment_flow.layout: One question per screen. Max-w-2xl centered column.
assessment_flow.header: Minimal. Back button on the top left. Progress text/bar on the top right.
assessment_flow.question: Large H2 (text-3xl+). Left aligned.
assessment_flow.options: Vertical stack of large, tappable cards (min-h-[80px]). No radio dots necessary; the entire card is the selection area. On select, highlight border and background with accent_muted.
assessment_flow.navigation: Sticky bottom bar or inline 'Next' button appearing/activating only after selection.

career_rating_page.item_structure: Flex container. Left: Career Title + 1 sentence context. Right: 5-star rating component.
career_rating_page.instructions: Make it feel like a professional review tool, not a generic survey.

results_page.layout: Grid layout. The #1 matched career spans full width (hero result). #2 and #3 sit side-by-side below.
results_page.top_match_card: Premium feel. Massive match percentage typography (e.g., '98% Match' in text-6xl). Includes Category, Description, Key Skills tags, and 'Why it matches'.
results_page.share_feature: A prominent 'Share Result' or 'Download as Image' button (using a library like html2canvas) styled distinctively.
results_page.persistence: Load the last result from localStorage if the user re-visits.

testing.mandate: ALL interactive elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute in kebab-case format. E.g., data-testid='start-assessment-button', data-testid='option-card-1', data-testid='next-question-button'.

image_urls.description: Woman using MacBook, professional and modern. Recommendation: Apply CSS grayscale and use alongside blue accents.
Minimalist white staircase rising. Perfect for background elements or the rating page header.
Professional writing/working. Can be used to anchor the top matched career result.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do NOT use standard Shadcn cards for the main marketing/landing pages. Use pure HTML/Tailwind with generous spacing.
Implement the assessment logic strictly with React state, passing data between steps. Persist final results to localStorage.
Ensure 'html-to-image' or 'html2canvas' is installed and implemented for the Share/Download Results requirement.
Apply Framer Motion layout transitions between question changes to make the one-question-per-screen UX feel incredibly smooth.
Strictly follow the color palette. No gradients unless it's a subtle background mesh. Rely on the bold Cobalt Blue for all primary actions.
Remember to include 'data-testid' on all buttons, assessment choices, and navigation elements.
